$comment: sidecar to actuator_output_v1.schema.json · NOT a replacement · v1 is LOCKED (per AGENTS.tape g11_vendored_snapshots_readonly). This v0 sidecar adds shear-response fields for the LVAD scenario ① shear-gated nanobot work; the locked v1 actuator schema is untouched.
title: raw_77_nanobot_shear_response_v0 — shear-coupling sidecar row
description: Per-skeleton, per-shear-stress row capturing Bell-model gating latch state and sub-second response time for LVAD-impeller shear-triggered payload release. Anchors §3 real-limits from LVAD/SHEAR_GATED_NANOBOT.tape (70-150 dyn/cm² LVAD vs 1-10 venous baseline, sub-second residence-time response, payload-leakage proxy via latch state). Author scope: in-silico simulator-consistency only (AGENTS.tape g8 / f2).

shear_stress_dyn_cm2.description: Applied wall shear stress in dyn/cm². Physiological venous/arterial 1-10; LVAD impeller 70-150 (HeartMate 3 axial zones); RBC hemolysis time-integral threshold ~400.

trigger_threshold_dyn_cm2.description: Designed shear threshold above which the gating latch opens. Default 50 dyn/cm² sits between venous baseline 10 and LVAD floor 70, giving ≥5× venous separation and ≤3× LVAD-floor margin.

gating_latch_state.description: Discrete latch state. closed = sub-threshold (no release); primed = within 0.5× of threshold (intermediate); open = supra-threshold Bell-model k_open ≥ baseline × e.

tau_response_s.description: Bell-model 1/k_open response time in seconds. Sub-second target (<1.0 s) to satisfy LVAD residence-time constraint (<1 s per impeller pass).

skeleton.description: Mechanical skeleton variant; same enumeration as actuator_output_v1.skeleton.

bell_model.description: Bell-model parameters used to compute k_open. k_open = k0 * exp(τ * x_β * A / kT).

k_open_per_s.description: Computed Bell-model opening rate at shear τ.
